channel.title: Dog on Здесь будет название
channel.description: Recent content in Dog on Здесь будет название

item.title: Brown Wolf
item.description: Brown Wolf came to them in the night, a shadow slipping into their California camp, half-starved and wary as any wild thing. Walt and Madge Irvine nursed him back to health, and in return, he gave them a loyalty that seemed absolute — until the day the call of the North reclaimed him.

item.title: The Sun-Dog Trail
item.description:
The sun-dog trail stretched across the frozen landscape, named for the optical phenomena that appeared on bright winter days — false suns flanking the real one, created by ice crystals in the atmosphere. The old-timers took sun dogs as a sign, though they disagreed about what the sign meant.

Sitka Charley was running the mail that winter, a hundred pounds of letters and packages destined for the mining camps along the Yukon. He was a native man, one of the few trusted with this crucial work, his knowledge of the land unmatched by any white man in the territory.

item.title: For the Love of a Man
item.description:
For the love of a man, Buck performed what might be considered a miracle. John Thornton saved Buck’s life, and in return, Buck gave him something that could not be measured — a devotion so complete, so absolute, that it transcended the ordinary bonds between man and dog.

They had found each other at the lowest point of Buck’s existence. The previous owners, Hal, Charles, and Mercedes, had driven the team to the breaking point. Dogs had died on the trail, worn out by overwork and underfeeding. Buck himself had been near death when Thornton intervened.

item.title: The Toil of Trace and Trail
item.description: The toil of trace and trail shaped them all — men and dogs alike. Day after day, they pushed forward through the frozen wilderness, carrying the mail that connected the isolated communities of the North. It was brutal work, but there was a strange beauty in it, a rhythm that became almost meditative.

item.title: Who Has Won to Mastership
item.description: Who has won to mastership — this was the question that hung over the team like a storm cloud waiting to break. Every dog knew it, sensed it in the tension between Buck and Spitz. The white dog’s reign was coming to an end; it was only a matter of when and how.

item.title: The Dominant Primordial Beast
item.description: The dominant primordial beast was strong in Buck, and under the fierce conditions of trail life it grew and grew. Yet it was a secret growth. His newborn cunning gave him poise and control. He was too busy adjusting himself to the new life to feel at ease, and not only did he not pick fights, but he avoided them whenever possible.

item.title: The Law of Club and Fang
item.description: The law of club and fang was the first lesson Buck learned in the harsh northern wilderness. It was a primitive law, ancient as the hills themselves, and it demanded absolute obedience. The man in the red sweater had taught him this lesson well, though the teaching had been brutal and unforgiving.
